strengths: The student has indicated which Approaches to Learning (ATL) skills are relevant to achieving the goal.
weaknesses: There are no concrete examples or evidence showing how ATL skills were applied in practice.
The impact of these skills on the quality of the final product is not discussed.
Opportunities to reflect on challenges in applying certain skills are not taken.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Identifying ATL skills is a good first step, but to strengthen this section the student needs to illustrate, with specific examples, how they used research, self-management, or communication skills during the project. Discussing the outcome of applying these skills will demonstrate their real value.

strengths: The student identified an intended product, demonstrating awareness of the taskâs overall purpose.
weaknesses: No detailed or measurable success criteria were articulated to guide the work.
There is no clear plan showing how tasks will be sequenced or what resources will be used.
Connections to personal interest and the learning goal remain undeveloped.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: While the report names what is to be produced, it lacks the specific, measurable criteria and sequencing required for a strong planning phase. To improve, the student should define detailed success criteria, map out a feasible timeline, and explain why the chosen product aligns with their interests.

strengths: The student acknowledges whether the intended product was completed.
weaknesses: There is no evaluation of the product against any defined success criteria.
The reflection does not explore the impact on personal learning or identify areas for growth.
Supporting evidence (such as examples, feedback received, or data) is entirely missing.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: A basic statement of completion is a starting point, but effective reflection requires systematically comparing the finished work to the original criteria and examining how the process contributed to the studentâs skills and understanding. Incorporating specific examples or external feedback will deepen the analysis.

annotations.comment: This document contains only supervision records and declarations. Please submit the actual mini report sections: planning (learning goal, intended product, detailed success criteria), application of ATL skills with evidence, and reflection against those criteria.
